title: HTML Tokens, Error Recovery, Parser Selection, and Differential Evidence
description: A selector works under one parser but fails under another because omitted elements, foster parenting, broken nesting, and script or noscript state produce different trees. Learners cumulatively produce bounded parser and tree-behavior comparison through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish source bytes, decoded text, tokens, parse errors, the constructed tree, serialized markup, and browser DOM state.
Predict omitted tags, mismatched nesting, duplicate attributes, character references, templates, foreign content, and recovery effects on target nodes.
Select html.parser, lxml HTML, lxml XML, or html5lib deliberately and pin that parser in code, tests, and metadata.
